nextcloudHelp.title: Nextcloud Setup Help
nextcloudHelp.description: Follow these steps to configure your Nextcloud server for browser access
nextcloudHelp.whyNeededDesc: Browsers have security rules that prevent websites from directly connecting to other servers. Your Nextcloud needs to give permission for this app to connect.
nextcloudHelp.apacheDesc: If your Nextcloud runs on Apache web server, you'll need to add CORS headers to the configuration.
nextcloudHelp.nginxDesc: If your Nextcloud runs on Nginx web server, you'll need to add CORS headers to the configuration.
nextcloudHelp.sslDesc: If you see SSL certificate errors, your HTTPS certificate may be invalid, expired, or self-signed. Self-signed certificates are not trusted by browsers.
nextcloudHelp.whereToAddDesc: You need SSH access to your server.
nextcloudHelp.afterConfigSteps: Restart your web server to apply changes
nextcloudHelp.certbotInstructions: For self-signed certificates, use Let's Encrypt with certbot for free SSL certificates
nextcloudHelp.corsBlocked: Connection blocked by browser security (CORS). Your Nextcloud server needs to allow browser connections.
nextcloudHelp.authFailed: Authentication failed. Please check your username and app password in Nextcloud settings.
nextcloudHelp.sslError: SSL Certificate Error. Your server's HTTPS certificate is invalid, expired, or untrusted.
nextcloudHelp.dialogDescription: Follow these simple steps to enable browser connections to your Nextcloud server
nextcloudHelp.enableModulesFirst: Step 1: Enable required Apache modules (run once):
nextcloudHelp.apacheInstructions: Step 2: Add this inside your <VirtualHost *:443> block:
nextcloudHelp.nginxInstructions: Add these lines to your Nginx server block:
nextcloudHelp.sslInstructions: Options to fix SSL certificate errors:
nextcloudHelp.testConnection: Then test the connection again from this app.
nextcloudHelp.syntaxOkDesc: Configuration is valid, proceed to restart
nextcloudHelp.ah00558Desc: Warning about ServerName - this is harmless, you can ignore it
nextcloudHelp.invalidCommandDesc: Module not enabled, run Step 1 again
nextcloudHelp.syntaxErrorDesc: Check for typos in the configuration
nextcloudHelp.options401Fix: Make sure the <Limit OPTIONS> block is inside the <LocationMatch> block
nextcloudHelp.corsHeaderMissingFix: Run: sudo a2enmod headers && sudo systemctl restart apache2
nextcloudHelp.rewriteNotWorkingFix: Run: sudo a2enmod rewrite && sudo systemctl restart apache2
nextcloudHelp.nginxTestOkDesc: All tests passed, safe to reload
nextcloudHelp.nginxUnknownDirectiveDesc: Check syntax - possibly a typo or missing semicolon
nextcloudHelp.nginxOptions401Fix: Ensure the OPTIONS block returns 204 before any auth checks
nextcloudHelp.nginxFindFileInstruction: List your Nginx site configuration files to find the one for your Nextcloud domain:
nextcloudHelp.nginxEditFileInstruction: Edit the file for your Nextcloud domain:
nextcloudHelp.selfSignedCert: Self-signed certificate - not trusted by browsers
nextcloudHelp.invalidCert: Invalid certificate - domain mismatch or configuration error
nextcloudHelp.howToCheckSsl: How to check your SSL certificate:
nextcloudHelp.openNextcloudUrl: Open your Nextcloud URL in a browser (e.g., {{url}})
nextcloudHelp.clickPadlock: Click the padlock icon in the address bar
nextcloudHelp.isExpired: Is it expired? Check the valid dates
nextcloudHelp.doesDomainMatch: Does the domain match? Certificate must match your URL
nextcloudHelp.isSelfSigned: Is it self-signed? Look for "Unknown Certificate Authority"
nextcloudHelp.howToFixSsl: How to fix SSL certificate issues:
nextcloudHelp.ifUsingLetsEncrypt: If using Let's Encrypt with Certbot:
nextcloudHelp.getFreeCert: Get a free trusted certificate from Let's Encrypt:
nextcloudHelp.sslFixNote: Note: After fixing SSL issues, you may need to restart your web server and clear your browser cache before reconnecting.
nextcloudHelp.gettingStartedDesc: New to Nextcloud? Follow these steps to connect:
nextcloudHelp.step1Desc: Your Nextcloud server URL is the address you use to access Nextcloud in your browser (e.g., https://cloud.example.com or https://your-domain.com/nextcloud).
nextcloudHelp.step2Desc: Use the same username you use to log into Nextcloud (usually shown in the top-right corner when logged in).
nextcloudHelp.step3CreatePassword: How to create an App Password:
nextcloudHelp.step3Instructions1: Log into your Nextcloud web interface
nextcloudHelp.step3Instructions2: Click your profile icon (top-right corner) → Settings
nextcloudHelp.step3Instructions3: Go to "Security" in the left sidebar
nextcloudHelp.step3Instructions5: Enter a name like "OwnJournal" and click "Create new app password"
nextcloudHelp.step3Instructions6: Copy the generated password (it's shown only once!)
nextcloudHelp.step3Tip: App passwords are special passwords that let apps connect to your Nextcloud without using your main password. You can revoke them anytime from the Security settings.
nextcloudHelp.generateAppPassword: Generate an app password in your Nextcloud security settings
nextcloudHelp.corsDescriptionPart1: Your Nextcloud server needs to be configured to allow browser connections. This is a
nextcloudHelp.mobileAppNoteDesc: Mobile apps may have stricter connection restrictions than desktop browsers. If connection works on your computer but not on your phone:
nextcloudHelp.mobileAppTip1: Ensure your Nextcloud server allows connections from any origin (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *)
nextcloudHelp.mobileAppTip2: Check that your SSL certificate is valid and trusted (not self-signed)
nextcloudHelp.mobileAppTip3: Some hosting providers block mobile app connections by default - contact your host if issues persist
nextcloudHelp.apacheEditConfig: Edit your Apache SSL VirtualHost config:
nextcloudHelp.apacheAddInside: Add the configuration INSIDE the {{tag}} block (before the closing {{closeTag}} tag)
nextcloudHelp.subdirectoryInstalls: For subdirectory installs, adjust the path (e.g., {{path}})
nextcloudHelp.nginxEditConfig: Edit your Nginx config file (usually in {{path}}) inside the {{block}} block for your Nextcloud site.
nextcloudHelp.letsEncryptFile: Edit your SSL config file ending in
nextcloudHelp.replaceFilename: Replace YOUR_FILE.conf with the filename from above (e.g., nextcloud-le-ssl.conf)
nextcloudHelp.simpleConfigDesc: Uses wildcard origin (*) - works with all web browsers and mobile apps. Best for personal Nextcloud servers.
nextcloudHelp.simpleConfigDescNginx: Uses wildcard origin (*) - works with all web browsers and mobile apps. Best for personal Nginx servers.
nextcloudHelp.secureConfigDesc: Uses origin reflection - only allows your specific app domains. Use this if your Nextcloud is shared with others.
nextcloudHelp.secureConfigDescNginx: Uses origin reflection - only allows your specific app domains. Use this if your Nginx server is shared with others.

deleteAccount.title: Delete Account
deleteAccount.description: Permanently delete your account and all associated data from our servers.
deleteAccount.warning: This will cancel your subscription, delete your profile, and remove all your data. This action cannot be undone.
deleteAccount.successDesc: Your account has been permanently deleted.
deleteAccount.failedDesc: Please try again later or contact support.
deleteAccount.doNotClose: Please keep this page open until deletion is complete

journalName.title: Journal Name
journalName.description: This name will appear in all exported PDF and Word files.
journalName.updatedDesc: Your journal name will be used in all exports.

dataManagement.cloudSyncDesc: Automatically keeps your journals synchronized across devices using encrypted cloud storage. Your data remains private and secure.
dataManagement.backupDesc: Create JSON backups of your journal data for safekeeping or migration.
dataManagement.cloudFeature4: Your data, your storage, zero knowledge
dataManagement.backupFeature3: Useful for migration and emergency recovery
dataManagement.backupNote: Backup creates a JSON file with all entries. Import restores from a previous backup file.
dataManagement.importHintDesc: Backups are saved to Documents/. Navigate there to find your JSON backup files.

ai.title: AI Features
ai.description: Powerful AI analysis for your journal entries
ai.privacyDesc: When you use AI features, your journal content is sent securely to third-party AI services for analysis. We do not store this data on our servers. Our AI providers may temporarily retain data for abuse monitoring but do not use it for model training.

storage.reconnectHint: If the problem continues, try disconnecting and reconnecting your storage in Settings.
storage.waitForSyncComplete: Please wait for the current sync to complete before changing encryption mode.
storage.pendingUploadsWarning: {{count}} entries are still being uploaded. Disconnecting now may cause data loss. Continue anyway?
storage.encryptionModeDesc: Choose how your journal entries are protected during sync
storage.simpleModeDesc: Entries sync to cloud as readable JSON files. Easier setup, no password needed.
storage.e2eModeDesc: Entries encrypted locally before cloud sync. Only you can decrypt with your password. Maximum privacy.
storage.cannotSwitchDesc: You have existing entries. Please delete all data before switching encryption modes.
storage.cannotSwitchFromE2E: Cannot switch from E2E mode - reset data first if needed
storage.cannotSwitchToE2E: To use E2E encryption, disconnect from cloud storage first or reset all data.
storage.disconnectFirst: Please disconnect from cloud storage or reset all data to change encryption mode.
storage.cannotVerifyDesc: Please enter your password first or reset all data.
storage.passwordRequiredDesc: You'll need to set a password to enable end-to-end encryption.
storage.passwordRequiredAfterMigration: Please enter your password to complete the storage provider switch and reload your encryption key.
storage.e2eEnabledDesc: Using your existing password for encryption.
storage.simpleModeEnabledDesc: Your entries will sync as readable JSON files.
storage.passwordStoredSecurely: Your password is securely stored on this device for automatic encryption.
storage.forgotPasswordOption1: Use 'Change Password' below if you know your current password
storage.forgotPasswordOption2: Export a backup first, then use 'DELETE All Data' in Danger Zone to start fresh
storage.changePasswordDesc: Change your journal password. All entries will be re-encrypted with the new password.
storage.resetAllDataDesc: Permanently delete all entries, encryption keys, and cached password
storage.resetAllDataWarning: This will permanently delete all your journal entries, encryption keys, and cached password. If you use E2E encryption, your entries will become permanently unreadable as the encryption key will be destroyed.
storage.resetAllDataConfirm: This action cannot be undone. Continue?
storage.backupRecommendation: We strongly recommend creating a backup before proceeding.
storage.backupInstructions: To create a backup: Click on your profile icon (top right) → Export Data
storage.allDataDeletedDesc: All entries, encryption keys, and cached password have been cleared. Encrypted data is now permanently inaccessible. You can start fresh.
storage.resetFailedDesc: Failed to delete all data. Please try again.
storage.syncFailedDesc: Failed to sync with cloud storage
storage.switchedToE2EDesc: Please enter your password to decrypt your entries.
storage.disconnectedForE2EDesc: Reconnect in E2E Encrypted mode to access your encrypted entries.
storage.startingFreshDesc: Cloud encrypted data will be cleared. You can now sync new entries in Simple mode.
storage.cloudCredentialsWarning: Cloud credentials stored in plaintext in device local storage
storage.deviceAccessWarning: Anyone with device access can read OAuth tokens
storage.recommendE2E: Recommended E2E mode for sensitive journals
storage.cloudFeature4: Your data, your storage, zero knowledge
storage.cloudStorageIntro: Connect to cloud storage providers to sync your journal across devices. You can connect to multiple providers for redundancy.
storage.activeProviderDesc: Choose which connected provider syncs your data. Only the active provider performs sync operations.
storage.primaryChanged: {{provider}} is now your active storage provider
storage.backupFeature3: Useful for migration and emergency recovery
storage.autoSyncAcrossDevices: • Automatically sync across all your devices
storage.secureEncryption: • Secure encryption with your chosen mode
storage.conflictResolution: • Automatic conflict resolution for simultaneous edits
storage.offlineSupport: • Full offline support with background sync
storage.cannotSwitchModeDesc: You have {{count}} existing entries. Please delete all data before switching encryption modes.
storage.cannotVerifyEntriesDesc: Please enter your password first or reset all data.
storage.passwordSavedDesc: Your password is stored securely and will be remembered.
storage.passwordNotSet: No password is currently set. Set a password to enable E2E encryption.
storage.passwordWillBeRequested: Password will be requested when connecting to cloud providers.
storage.clearPasswordConfirm:
This will remove the stored encryption password from this device. You'll need to enter your password again the next time you open the app.

Your encrypted data will not be affected.

Continue?
storage.changePasswordConfirm:
Are you sure?

This will decrypt all your entries with the old password and re-encrypt them with the new password.

Make sure you remember your new password - it cannot be recovered if forgotten.

Continue?
storage.finalConfirm:
Final Confirmation

This will permanently delete ALL your journal entries from your device and cloud storage.

⚠️ RECOMMENDED: Create a backup before proceeding!
To create a backup: Click on your profile icon (top right) → Export Data

Are you absolutely sure you want to continue?
storage.passwordPersistenceError: Failed to update password storage setting

passwordPersistence.title: Password Storage
passwordPersistence.description: Choose how your encryption password is stored on this device. More secure options require re-entering your password more often.
passwordPersistence.localStorageHint: Your password is encrypted and stored persistently. Most convenient for personal devices.
passwordPersistence.sessionStorageHint: Password cleared when you close the browser tab. Recommended for shared computers.
passwordPersistence.noneHint: You'll need to enter your password every time you access your journal. Recommended for public computers.

storage.connectStorageFirst: Connect to cloud storage to set up encryption, or switch to Simple mode.
storage.cloudStorageDesc: Connect to your preferred cloud storage provider. All data is {{mode}}.
storage.dangerZoneDesc: Permanently delete all journal entries, encryption keys, and cached password. This action cannot be undone.
storage.googleDriveDesc: Sync your encrypted journal entries with Google Drive.
storage.googleConnectedDesc: Your journal entries will sync automatically.
storage.googleSyncActive: Your journal is syncing with Google Drive
storage.icloudSyncActive: Your journal is syncing with iCloud
storage.googleClientIdMissing: Google Drive is not configured. Missing client ID.
storage.googleSimpleModeWarning: Credentials stored without encryption in simple mode
storage.dropboxDesc: Sync your encrypted journal entries with Dropbox.

storage.dropboxSyncActive: Your journal is syncing with Dropbox
storage.dropboxClientIdMissing: Dropbox is not configured. Missing client ID.
storage.dropboxSimpleModeWarning: Credentials stored without encryption in simple mode

storage.providerConnectedInactive: Connected but not actively syncing. Use the dropdown above to set as active.
storage.switchToPrimaryToSync: Switch to this provider in the dropdown above to start syncing
storage.providerTransferDesc: Connect at least 2 cloud providers to transfer data between them.
storage.transferBetweenProvidersDesc: Copy all entries from one provider to another.
storage.multipleProvidersWarningDesc: Only the primary provider actively syncs data. Secondary providers are only for migration purposes. Use migration to transfer data, then disconnect inactive providers.
storage.migrationCompleteDisconnectDesc: All entries have been copied to {{target}}. Would you like to disconnect {{source}} to avoid confusion?
storage.activeSyncDesc: This storage is actively syncing your data
storage.inactiveSyncDesc: Not syncing - use migration to transfer data here
storage.primaryDesc: This storage is used for reading data
storage.noKeyOnProviderDesc: {{provider}} doesn't have your encryption key. Use migration or copy the key first.
storage.providerVerifyFailed: Could not check if {{provider}} has your encryption key.
storage.copyKeyExplanation: Copy your encryption key from the active provider to another connected provider so you can switch between them.
storage.keyCopiedDesc: Your encryption key has been copied to {{provider}}. You can now switch to this provider.
storage.noKeyToCopy: No encryption key available to copy
storage.copyKeyFirst: This provider doesn't have your encryption key. Use 'Copy Encryption Key' or transfer data first.
storage.connectProvider: Connect a cloud storage provider in Settings to enable automatic sync

encryption.journalPasswordDesc: Set your journal password used to encrypt entries on this device.
encryption.passwordSetDesc: Your journal password is now active.
encryption.changePasswordDesc: Change the password used to protect your journal. All entries will be re-encrypted with the new password.
encryption.passwordChangedDesc: Your encryption password has been updated. Use this new password on all your devices.
encryption.changeFailedDesc: Failed to change password. Please check your current password.
encryption.deleteAllEntriesDesc: Permanently delete all journal entries from local storage and cloud. This action cannot be undone.
encryption.warningPermanentDesc: If you use E2E encryption, your entries will become permanently unreadable as the encryption key will be destroyed.
encryption.allEntriesDeletedDesc: Your journal has been completely cleared.
encryption.passwordClearedDesc: Stored encryption password removed. You'll need to enter it on next login.
encryption.incorrectPasswordDesc: The password you entered is incorrect. Please try again.
encryption.e2eExplanation: Your journal entries will be encrypted on your device before syncing to the cloud.
encryption.chooseStrongPassword: Choose a strong password. It cannot be recovered if lost — make sure you remember it or store it safely.
encryption.saltNotFoundDesc: Your encryption setup data was not found. This can happen if browser data was cleared or you're using a new device.
encryption.saltNotFoundAction: You can reset your encryption setup to start fresh. Your cloud data will remain encrypted and accessible with the correct password.
encryption.decryptionFailedDesc: The password you entered doesn't match the encryption key stored in the cloud.
encryption.decryptionFailedHint: If this is a new device, make sure you're using the same password from your original device.
encryption.newDeviceHint: Enter the same password you used when first setting up encryption.
encryption.clearLocalDataDesc: Clear local encryption data to start fresh. Your cloud data remains safe.
encryption.localDataClearedDesc: Please enter your password to sync with cloud storage.
encryption.networkErrorDesc: Could not connect to cloud storage. Please check your connection and try again.
encryption.entriesWithoutKeyDesc: Your cloud storage has encrypted entries but the encryption key is missing. This may happen if the key was deleted.
encryption.cloudKeyRequiredDesc: Your entries were encrypted with a cloud key. Connect to your cloud storage to decrypt them.
encryption.encryptedEntriesNeedCloudDesc: You have encrypted entries from a previous sync. Reconnect to the same cloud storage with your encryption password to access them.
encryption.reconnectToAccessEntries: Reconnect to your cloud storage to access your encrypted entries. They were synced with E2E encryption and need the same storage connection.
encryption.migrateEntriesDesc: {{count}} entries are not encrypted. Migrate them to E2E encryption for full security.
encryption.resetToSimpleMode: Switched to Simple mode. Set a new password to re-enable E2E encryption.

googleDrive.title: Google Drive Storage
googleDrive.description: Sync your encrypted journal entries with Google Drive (app folder only).

icloud.afterSignedInDesc: After completing sign-in in the popup, click below to continue.

syncStatus.highFailureRateDesc: {{rate}}% of sync operations are failing. This may indicate connection issues or storage provider problems.
syncStatus.frequentConflictsDesc: {{rate}} conflicts per 100 syncs detected. Consider reducing simultaneous editing across devices.
syncStatus.recentConflictSpikeDesc: Conflict rate increased significantly in the last hour. Multiple devices may be editing simultaneously.
syncStatus.circuitBreakersActiveDesc: Some storage operations are temporarily blocked due to repeated failures. The system will auto-recover.
syncStatus.multipleRecentFailuresDesc: {{count}} sync attempts failed in the last 5 minutes. Check your connection and storage provider.
syncStatus.reduceFailuresDesc: Check your internet connection and storage provider credentials. Consider changing providers if issues persist.
syncStatus.minimizeConcurrentEditsDesc: Avoid editing the same journal on multiple devices at the same time. Wait for sync to complete before switching devices.
syncStatus.waitForCircuitBreakerDesc: Temporary limits will auto-reset after a cooldown period. No action required.

syncHealth.title: Sync Health Monitor
syncHealth.description: Real-time monitoring and alerts for your sync system
syncHealth.greenMetrics: • Green metrics indicate healthy sync operations
syncHealth.yellowWarnings: • Yellow warnings suggest potential issues to watch
syncHealth.checkDiagnostics: • Check Diagnostics tab for detailed operation logs
syncHealth.highFailureRateDesc: {{rate}}% of sync operations are failing. This may indicate network issues or cloud storage problems.
syncHealth.frequentConflictsDesc: {{rate}} conflicts per 100 syncs detected. This suggests simultaneous editing across multiple devices.
syncHealth.recentConflictSpikeDesc: {{count}} conflicts detected in the last hour. You may be actively using multiple devices.
syncHealth.circuitBreakersActiveDesc: {{count}} safety pause(s) are active due to repeated failures. Sync operations are temporarily paused.
syncHealth.multipleRecentFailuresDesc: {{count}} out of the last 10 operations failed. This indicates an ongoing issue.
syncHealth.reduceFailuresDesc: Check your internet connection and cloud storage access. Consider temporarily disabling auto-sync during poor network conditions.
syncHealth.reduceFailuresAction: Check Diagnostics tab for detailed error logs
syncHealth.minimizeConcurrentEditsDesc: Try to avoid editing the same entries on multiple devices simultaneously. Wait for sync to complete before switching devices.
syncHealth.minimizeConcurrentEditsAction: View Conflicts tab to see resolved conflicts
syncHealth.waitForCircuitBreakerDesc: Safety pauses automatically reset after 5 minutes. Check your cloud provider connection and credentials in the Storage tab.
syncHealth.systemHealthyDesc: Your sync is working smoothly. Continue monitoring for any changes in sync patterns.

syncDiagnostics.title: Sync Diagnostics
syncDiagnostics.diagnosticsNote: Diagnostics are stored in memory and reset on page reload. Max 200 entries kept.
syncDiagnostics.noDiagnosticsYet: No diagnostics yet. Perform a sync to see logs.

cloudEncrypted.title: Encrypted Data Detected
cloudEncrypted.description: Your cloud storage contains encrypted journal entries from a previous E2E setup.
cloudEncrypted.cannotAccessInSimple: These entries were created with end-to-end encryption and cannot be read in Simple mode.
cloudEncrypted.reconnectE2EDesc: Disconnect your storage now, then reconnect using E2E Encrypted mode to access your encrypted entries.
cloudEncrypted.startFreshDesc: Delete encrypted data from cloud and continue with Simple mode. Encrypted entries will be lost.
cloudEncrypted.warningDesc: This will permanently delete all encrypted data from your cloud storage. This cannot be undone.

welcome.title: Welcome to OwnJournal
welcome.body: A private sanctuary for your thoughts. Your data is encrypted and yours alone.
welcome.desc: A private sanctuary for your thoughts. Your data is encrypted and yours alone.

storage.title: Where should we keep your journal?
storage.subtitle: Select where your journal will be saved
storage.hint: You can change this later in Settings

welcome.title: Welcome to OwnJournal!
welcome.content: Your private, encrypted journal. Let's take a quick 30-second tour!

timeline.title: Your Journal Timeline
timeline.content: Your journal entries will appear here, organized by date. Click the + button to create your first entry.

syncStatus.title: Sync Status
syncStatus.content: Once you connect cloud storage, this indicator shows your sync status. Green means synced!

encryptionModes.title: Choose Your Security Level
encryptionModes.content:
Let's set up your encryption mode.

• Simple Mode: Quick and easy to start

• E2E Encrypted: Maximum security with your own password

Open Settings from this button to change your encryption mode.

settings.title: Connect Cloud Storage
settings.content:
Benefits of cloud connection:

• Sync across devices
• Automatic backup
• Access from anywhere

Open Settings from this button to connect your cloud storage.

help.title: Help & Support
help.content: Need help anytime? Click here to access guides, tips, and restart this tour.

complete.title: You're All Set!
complete.content: Start writing your first entry, or open Settings to connect cloud storage for syncing across devices.

help.title: Help Center
help.description: Find answers to common questions and learn how to get the most out of OwnJournal

gettingStarted.tourDesc: New to OwnJournal? Take a quick guided tour of the app.

firstEntry.title: Creating Your First Entry
firstEntry.step1: 1. Tap the + button at the top of the timeline
firstEntry.step2: 2. Write your journal entry in the editor—Markdown formatting is supported
firstEntry.step3: 3. Add tags and mood, then tap Save

syncSetup.title: Setting Up Cloud Sync
syncSetup.step1: 1. Open Settings and go to Storage tab
syncSetup.step2: 2. Choose your preferred cloud provider (Google Drive, Dropbox, or Nextcloud)
syncSetup.step3: 3. Follow the authentication flow to connect

accountSetup.title: Creating Your Account
accountSetup.desc: You can sign up using email/password or social login:
accountSetup.emailOption: Email & Password: Enter your email and choose a password
accountSetup.googleOption: Google: Click 'Continue with Google' for quick sign-up
accountSetup.appleOption: Apple: Click 'Continue with Apple' for privacy-focused sign-up
accountSetup.tip: Tip: You can reset your password anytime using the 'Forgot password?' link

support.title: Contact Support
support.desc: Need help? We're here for you.

beforeContacting.title: Before Contacting Support
beforeContacting.desc: You might find a quick answer in our FAQ or Troubleshooting sections. Many common questions are already covered there.

email.title: Email Support
email.desc: For issues not covered in the help guide, please email us. Include your device type and a brief description of the issue to help us assist you faster.

encryptionArchitecture.title: How Your Data Is Protected
encryptionArchitecture.desc: OwnJournal uses industry-standard encryption to keep your journal private.

algorithm.title: Encryption Standard
algorithm.desc: Your data is encrypted using AES-256-GCM, the same encryption standard used by banks, governments, and security professionals worldwide. The 256-bit key length provides military-grade protection.

keyDerivation.title: Password Protection
keyDerivation.desc: Your password is never stored directly. Instead, it's transformed into an encryption key using PBKDF2 with 100,000 iterations—a process designed to make brute-force attacks impractical.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.step1: 1. Your password creates a unique encryption key (using PBKDF2)
howItWorks.step2: 2. A random master key encrypts each journal entry
howItWorks.step3: 3. The master key is protected by your password-derived key
howItWorks.step4: 4. Only encrypted data is synced to your cloud storage

encryptionArchitecture.summary: This layered approach means even if someone accesses your cloud storage, they cannot read your entries without your password.

encryptionModes.title: Understanding Encryption Modes
encryptionModes.simpleDesc: Your data is synced to cloud storage without encryption. Easier to set up, but your cloud provider can see your data.
encryptionModes.e2eDesc: Your data is encrypted before leaving your device. Only you can read your journal - not even your cloud provider.

zeroKnowledge.title: Zero Knowledge Architecture
zeroKnowledge.desc: With E2E encryption enabled, your encryption key never leaves your device. We cannot access your data even if we wanted to - that's Zero Knowledge.

passwordTips.title: Password Tips
passwordTips.tip1: Choose a strong, memorable password you won't forget
passwordTips.tip2: Consider using a passphrase—multiple words are easier to remember and more secure
passwordTips.tip3: We recommend storing your password in a password manager for safekeeping
passwordTips.warning: Important: If you forget your E2E password, your encrypted data cannot be recovered. We recommend keeping a backup in a safe place.

googleDrive.title: Connecting Google Drive
googleDrive.step1: 1. Tap 'Connect' next to Google Drive in Settings
googleDrive.step2: 2. Sign in with your Google account and grant permissions
googleDrive.note: Note: OwnJournal stores data in a hidden app folder, not visible in your regular Drive

dropbox.title: Connecting Dropbox
dropbox.step1: 1. Tap 'Connect' next to Dropbox in Settings
dropbox.step2: 2. Sign in and authorize the app

nextcloud.title: Connecting Nextcloud
nextcloud.step2: 2. Create an app password in Nextcloud settings
nextcloud.step3: 3. Enter your username and app password

transfer.title: Transferring Between Providers
transfer.desc: You can transfer your data between cloud providers without losing any entries.
transfer.steps: To transfer your data, open Settings, go to Storage, and select the Transfer option.

tags.title: Tags & Organization
tags.desc: Use tags to organize your entries by topic. Tag common themes to easily find related entries later.

tagSuggestions.title: AI Tag Suggestions
tagSuggestions.desc: AI analyzes your entry content and suggests relevant tags. This saves you time by automating the organization of your entries.

titleSuggestions.title: AI Title Suggestions
titleSuggestions.desc: AI suggests title options based on your entry content. Choose from multiple suggestions to find the one that fits best.

aiAnalysis.title: AI Analysis
aiAnalysis.desc: Get AI-powered insights about your journal entries, including mood analysis, topic detection, and personalized suggestions to deepen your self-reflection.

trends.title: Trend Analysis
trends.desc: Discover patterns in your journaling over time. Track mood trends, writing frequency, and common themes to better understand your habits.

export.title: Exporting Your Journal
export.desc: Export your journal in multiple formats:
export.pdf: Beautiful formatted PDF with cover page
export.proNote: PDF and Word exports are Plus features. JSON export is free.
export.saveLocationMobile: Mobile (Android/iOS): Files are saved to Documents/ on your device. A notification appears with a Share button to save elsewhere or send via email.
export.saveLocationWeb: Web browser: Files are automatically downloaded to your browser's default download folder.
export.shareHint: On mobile, tap the Share button in the notification to choose a different save location or share the file.

offlineBackup.title: Offline Backup
offlineBackup.desc: Save your journal as a JSON file directly to your device. This works completely offline, making it perfect for creating cloud-free backups or transferring your data.

backupImportExport.title: Backup & Restore
backupImportExport.desc: Export your journal as a JSON file for backup or import from a previous backup to restore your entries.
backupImportExport.exportDesc: To export a backup of your data, tap your profile picture and select Export Data. On mobile, files are saved to Documents/ with a Share option. On web, files download to your browser's Downloads folder.
backupImportExport.importMobile: Mobile (Android/iOS): When importing, navigate to Documents/ to find your backup files.
backupImportExport.importWeb: Web browser: Select the JSON backup file from wherever you saved it.

selfHostedStorage.title: Self-Hosted Storage
selfHostedStorage.desc: Connect to your own Nextcloud server for complete control over where your data is stored. Your server, your rules.

e2eEncryption.title: End-to-End Encryption
e2eEncryption.desc: Your journal entries are encrypted on your device before syncing or storage. Only you hold the encryption keys, so your data stays protected both at rest and in transit—no one else can read it.

zeroKnowledge.title: Zero-Knowledge Architecture
zeroKnowledge.desc: OwnJournal is designed so that we cannot access your journal content. With E2E encryption enabled, only you hold the password needed to decrypt your data. Optional AI features send content only when you use them (see Privacy Policy).

multiDeviceSync.title: Multi-Device Sync
multiDeviceSync.desc: Keep your journal in sync across all your devices. Automatic conflict resolution ensures you never lose an entry, even when editing on multiple devices.

syncIssues.title: Sync Not Working
syncIssues.tip2: Try clicking the sync button manually
syncIssues.tip3: Disconnect and reconnect your cloud storage

nextcloudEncryption.title: Nextcloud Encryption Conflict
nextcloudEncryption.intro: Nextcloud has its own server-side encryption that can interfere with OwnJournal. When this is enabled, Nextcloud encrypts your files on the server, and OwnJournal receives scrambled data instead of your journal entries.
nextcloudEncryption.why: Your Nextcloud administrator (or you, if you self-host) has enabled server-side encryption. This means Nextcloud encrypts files before storing them. When OwnJournal tries to read them via WebDAV, it gets encrypted data it can't understand.
nextcloudEncryption.fixOption1Desc: If you manage your own Nextcloud server, you need to use the terminal (SSH) because the checkbox in the admin panel cannot be unchecked while files are encrypted:
nextcloudEncryption.fixOption1Step1: 1. Open a terminal on your Nextcloud server
nextcloudEncryption.fixOption1Step2: 2. Go to your Nextcloud folder (e.g. cd /var/www/nextcloud)
nextcloudEncryption.fixOption1Step3: 3. Ensure maintenance mode is off. If it is on, run: sudo -u www-data php occ maintenance:mode --off
nextcloudEncryption.fixOption1Step4: 4. Decrypt all files: sudo -u www-data php occ encryption:decrypt-all (Nextcloud will put itself in maintenance mode during decryption and turn encryption off when finished.)
nextcloudEncryption.fixOption1Step5: 5. Encryption is disabled automatically when decryption finishes.
nextcloudEncryption.fixOption1Step6: 6. Nextcloud turns off maintenance mode automatically when done.
nextcloudEncryption.fixOption1Note: This can take a while depending on how many files you have. If you're not the server admin, ask them to run these commands or exclude the OwnJournal folder from encryption.
nextcloudEncryption.fixOption2Desc: If your Nextcloud has the "End-to-End Encryption" app enabled:
nextcloudEncryption.fixOption2Step1: 1. In Nextcloud, go to Apps > Security
nextcloudEncryption.fixOption2Step3: 3. Or simply make sure the OwnJournal folder is not marked for E2E encryption
nextcloudEncryption.importantNote: OwnJournal has its own E2E encryption that works independently. You don't need Nextcloud's encryption — your data is already encrypted by OwnJournal before it leaves your device.

passwordRecovery.title: Forgot E2E Password
passwordRecovery.warning: Unfortunately, E2E encrypted data cannot be recovered without the password.
passwordRecovery.desc: This is a core security feature of true end-to-end encryption—only you hold the key, which means even we cannot access your data. While this protects your privacy, it also means there's no way to recover encrypted entries without the password.
passwordRecovery.action1: If you have an unencrypted JSON backup, you can restore your entries from it.
passwordRecovery.action2: To start fresh, use the 'Delete All' option in the Danger Zone section of Settings to reset and create a new encrypted journal.

dataLocation.question: Where is my data stored?
dataLocation.answer: Your journal data is stored in your chosen cloud storage provider (Google Drive, Dropbox, or Nextcloud). OwnJournal does not store journals on its servers. With E2E encryption enabled, data is encrypted on your device before being uploaded.

offline.question: Can I use OwnJournal offline?
offline.answer: Yes! OwnJournal works completely offline. Your entries are stored locally on your device and automatically sync to cloud storage when you're back online.

devices.question: How many devices can I use?
devices.answer: You can use OwnJournal on as many devices as you like. Your encrypted journals sync across all your devices through your cloud storage provider.

free.question: Is OwnJournal free?
free.answer: Yes, the core journaling features are free. Plus unlocks AI features and trend analysis.

pro.question: What does Plus include?
pro.answer: Plus includes AI analysis, title suggestions, tag suggestions, trend analysis, and PDF/Word export.

manageSubscription.question: How do I manage my subscription?
manageSubscription.answer: Open Settings, go to the Account tab, scroll to the Subscription section, and click 'Manage' to access the billing portal. From there you can update your payment method, view your plan details, and manage billing settings.

invoices.question: How do I view my invoices?
invoices.answer: Open Settings, go to the Account tab, scroll to the Subscription section, and click 'Manage' to open the billing portal. Your complete invoice history is available there, and you can download invoices as PDF for your records.

cancelSubscription.question: How do I cancel my subscription?
cancelSubscription.answer: Open Settings, go to the Account tab, scroll to the Subscription section, and click 'Manage' to access the billing portal. Click 'Cancel plan' to stop your subscription. You'll retain Plus access until the end of your current billing period. Core journaling features remain free forever.

acceptance.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.content: By accessing or using OwnJournal, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use our service.

description.title: 2. Description of Service
description.content: OwnJournal is a personal journaling application operated by {{companyName}}, a company registered in {{companyCountry}}. It allows you to create, store, and manage your journal entries. We provide end-to-end encryption options and support various cloud storage providers for syncing your data across devices. Plus subscribers have access to optional AI-powered features such as mood analysis and suggestions; AI processing occurs only when these features are explicitly used.

accounts.title: 3. User Accounts
accounts.content: You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials and encryption passwords. You agree to accept responsibility for all activities that occur under your account. If you use E2E encryption, you are solely responsible for remembering your encryption password—we cannot recover encrypted data if you lose your password.

dataStorage.title: 4. Data Storage & Privacy
dataStorage.content: Your journal entries are stored in your chosen cloud storage provider (Google Drive, Dropbox, or Nextcloud). With E2E encryption enabled, your data is encrypted on your device before being uploaded. We do not have access to your encrypted content. In Simple mode, data is synced without encryption. Please refer to our Privacy Policy for detailed information about how we handle your data.

intellectualProperty.title: 5. Intellectual Property
intellectualProperty.content: You retain all rights to your journal content. The OwnJournal application, including its design, code, and branding, is owned by us and protected by intellectual property laws. You may not copy, modify, or distribute the application without permission.

limitation.title: 6. Limitation of Liability
limitation.content: OwnJournal is provided "as is" without warranties of any kind. We are not liable for any data loss, service interruptions, or damages arising from your use of the application, except to the extent such liability cannot be excluded or limited under applicable law. Nothing in these terms excludes or limits our liability for gross negligence, willful misconduct, or any other liability that cannot be legally excluded. You use the service at your own risk.

termination.title: 7. Termination
termination.content: We reserve the right to suspend or terminate your account if you violate these terms. You may delete your account at any time through the application settings.

changes.title: 8. Changes to Terms
changes.content: We may update these terms from time to time. Continued use of OwnJournal after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms. We will notify users of significant changes through the application.

contact.title: 9. Contact
contact.content: If you have questions about these terms, please contact us at {{companyEmail}}.

governingLaw.title: 10. Governing Law
governingLaw.content: These terms are governed by the laws of {{companyCountry}}. Any disputes shall be resolved in Swedish courts. This does not affect any mandatory consumer protection rights you may have under the laws of your country of residence.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.content: Your privacy is important to us. OwnJournal is operated by {{companyName}}, a company registered in {{companyCountry}}. This Privacy Policy explains how OwnJournal collects, uses, and protects your information. We designed OwnJournal with privacy as a core principle.

dataController.title: Data Controller
dataController.content: The data controller for OwnJournal is {{companyName}}. For data protection inquiries, you can contact us at {{companyEmail}}.

collection.title: Information We Collect
collection.content: The personal information we collect is minimal—your email address for authentication purposes, and your display name if provided by an external authentication provider (such as Google). Display names are used only for authentication and account management purposes and are never made public. In addition, we collect non-personally-identifiable, aggregated usage statistics such as page views, device type, country, and referrer source. This analytics data does not use cookies, does not track individual users, and cannot be linked to your account. When you use E2E encryption, your journal content is encrypted on your device and we cannot read it. In Simple mode, your journal data is synced to your chosen cloud provider without end-to-end encryption. Simple mode is intended for convenience and may reduce privacy compared to E2E mode; your data remains protected by your cloud provider's standard security measures.

usage.title: How We Use Information
usage.content: We use your email address solely for account authentication and important service notifications. We do not sell your personal information or share it with third parties for marketing purposes.

storage.title: Data Storage
storage.content: Your journal entries are stored in your chosen cloud storage provider (Google Drive, Dropbox, or Nextcloud)—not on our servers. You maintain full control over where your data is stored. We only store your account information needed for authentication.

encryption.title: End-to-End Encryption
encryption.content: When you enable E2E encryption, your journal entries are encrypted on your device before they leave it. Only you hold the encryption keys. Your data remains protected during storage and transfer, and cannot be read by anyone without your encryption password.

zeroKnowledge.title: Zero-Knowledge Architecture
zeroKnowledge.content: OwnJournal is designed so that we cannot access or read your journal content. With E2E encryption enabled, only you hold the password needed to decrypt your data—this is known as zero-knowledge architecture. If you use optional AI features, content is sent as described in "AI Features and Processing" above.

thirdParty.title: Third-Party Services
thirdParty.content: We integrate with cloud storage providers (Google Drive, Dropbox, Nextcloud) for data synchronization. These services have their own privacy policies. We use authentication services for secure sign-in. When you use optional AI features, data is processed by our AI service provider. Each third-party service operates under its own terms and privacy practices.

syncIdentifier.title: Sync and device identifier
syncIdentifier.content: For syncing your journal across devices and resolving edit conflicts, the app creates and uses a sync identifier. This identifier is stored on your device and in your cloud storage; it is not used to identify you across other apps or for advertising.

aiProcessing.title: AI Features and Processing
aiProcessing.content: OwnJournal offers optional AI-powered features such as mood analysis and suggestions, available exclusively to Plus subscribers. AI processing occurs only when you, as a Plus subscriber, explicitly use these features. When you use AI features, the relevant text is decrypted on your device first, then sent to the AI service through your explicit action; your journal content is sent to our AI providers — currently Google (Gemini) or OpenAI — via their APIs. OwnJournal does not store AI inputs or outputs on our servers. These providers may retain prompts and outputs for a limited period for abuse monitoring and policy enforcement, per their current policies. AI processing may occur outside the EEA; both Google and OpenAI maintain appropriate data transfer safeguards such as Standard Contractual Clauses. OwnJournal does not opt in to any provider programs that allow AI data to be used for model training or improvement. You can choose not to use AI features, and doing so does not affect the core journaling functionality.

diagnostics.title: Diagnostics and Crash Logs
diagnostics.content: We do not currently collect crash logs or send diagnostic data to our servers. The in-app Sync Diagnostics are stored only in memory on your device and are not transmitted to us. If we introduce crash reporting or server-side diagnostics in the future, we will update this policy.

retention.title: Data Retention
retention.content: Your journal data remains in your cloud storage until you delete it. Account information is retained while your account is active. You can delete your account at any time, which will remove your account data from our authentication system.

accountDeletion.title: Account deletion
accountDeletion.content: You can delete your account at any time via Settings in the app or by contacting us at {{companyEmail}}. Account deletion removes your profile, subscription, and authentication data from our systems. Journal data in your cloud storage remains under your control and can be deleted there by you.

legalBasis.title: Legal Basis for Processing
legalBasis.content: We process your data based on: (1) Contract performance - to provide the journaling service you signed up for; (2) Legitimate interests - to maintain service security and prevent abuse; (3) Consent - for optional features like AI analysis. You can withdraw consent for optional features at any time.

dpo.title: Data Protection Officer (DPO)
dpo.content: We have not appointed a Data Protection Officer because, based on our current operations, we are not required to do so under Article 37 of the GDPR. OwnJournal does not process or store journal content on our servers, and our processing is limited to minimal account, authentication, and subscription information. Journal content remains end-to-end encrypted and under the user's control. We will reassess this obligation if our processing activities change.

rights.title: Your Rights
rights.content: Under GDPR and similar privacy laws, you have the right to: access your data, export your data (portability), correct inaccurate data, delete your data (erasure), restrict processing, object to processing, and lodge a complaint with your local data protection authority. For EU residents, you may contact your national supervisory authority. In {{companyCountry}}, this is {{supervisoryAuthority}} at {{supervisoryAuthorityUrl}}. To exercise these rights, contact us at {{companyEmail}}.

children.title: Children's Privacy
children.content: OwnJournal is not intended for children under 13. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children. If you believe we have inadvertently collected such information, please contact us.

changes.title: Changes to This Policy
changes.content: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify you of significant changes through the application. Continued use after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.content: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy, wish to exercise your data rights, or have any concerns, please contact us at {{companyEmail}}. We aim to respond within 30 days.

recovery.title: Recover encrypted entries
recovery.description: Some entries could not be decrypted.
recovery.decryptionFailures: Some entries could not be decrypted
recovery.decryptionFailuresDesc: {{count}} of {{total}} entries failed to decrypt. Your password may have changed.
recovery.explanation: This usually happens when the encryption password was changed. Enter your original password to recover the entries.
recovery.hint1: • This is the password you first used to set up encryption
recovery.hint2: • Your encrypted entries are NOT lost - they just need the correct password
recovery.hint3: • If you cannot remember the password, entries may be unrecoverable
recovery.wrongPassword: Incorrect password. Please try again with your original encryption password.
recovery.keyCorrupted: The encryption key on all connected providers cannot decrypt your entries. If you used Google Drive, check its version history for a previous version of encryption-key.json in the OwnJournal folder.
recovery.noCloudKey: No encryption key found in cloud storage. The data may have been deleted.
recovery.entriesWithoutKey: Cloud storage has encrypted entries but the encryption key is missing. Please use the recovery option to restore access.
recovery.noCachedKey: No encryption data found locally. Connect to cloud storage to recover your entries.
recovery.networkError: Network error. Please check your connection and try again.
recovery.rateLimited: The cloud storage is temporarily limiting requests. Please wait 30-60 seconds and try again.
recovery.deleteLink: Or remove the unrecoverable entries permanently
recovery.confirmDelete: This will permanently delete {{count}} entries that cannot be decrypted. This cannot be undone.
recovery.deleteFailed: Failed to remove entries. Please try again.
recovery.keyMismatchExplanation: The encryption key that can decrypt these entries does not match the one currently in use. You can try reloading the key from cloud storage.
recovery.keyMismatchRecoveryHint: You may be able to recover the original key from your cloud storage version history (e.g. Google Drive or Nextcloud file versions for encryption-key.json in the OwnJournal folder).
recovery.keyMismatchHint1: • The encryption key in cloud storage does not match the key used to encrypt these entries
recovery.keyMismatchHint2: • Check Google Drive or Nextcloud version history for an older encryption-key.json
recovery.keyMismatchHint3: • If another device still has the correct key cached, connect from that device first
recovery.reloadKeySuccessDesc: Encryption key reloaded from cloud. Your entries should now be visible.
recovery.reloadKeyFailed: No connected cloud storage has a key that can decrypt these entries. Check version history for encryption-key.json.

validation.serverUrlTooLong: Server URL must be less than 255 characters
validation.ipNotAllowed: IP addresses are not allowed. Please use a domain name.
validation.usernameTooLong: Username must be less than 200 characters
validation.usernameInvalidChars: Username can only contain letters, numbers, and ._@-
validation.appPasswordTooLong: App password must be less than 500 characters

cloudErrors.notFound: Not found - The target location doesn't exist
cloudErrors.notFoundWithFile: Not found - The target location doesn't exist. File: {{file}}
cloudErrors.permissionDenied: Permission denied - You don't have permission to access this location
cloudErrors.authFailed: Authentication failed - Please check your credentials or reconnect
cloudErrors.serverError: Server error - The server encountered an internal error
cloudErrors.serverUnavailable: Server unavailable - Please try again later
cloudErrors.storageFull: Storage full - Insufficient space on server
cloudErrors.rateLimited: Rate limited - Too many requests, please wait
cloudErrors.encryptionError: Encryption error - Server-side encryption is preventing access
cloudErrors.connectionFailed: Connection failed - Please check your internet connection
cloudErrors.tokenRefreshFailed: Session expired - Please reconnect your cloud storage
cloudErrors.networkErrorCheckingKey: Temporary network error — please try again in a moment

incompatibleKey.title: Encryption Key Incompatible
incompatibleKey.description: The encryption key file stored in {{provider}} is corrupted or from a different installation.
incompatibleKey.cause1: An old installation left files behind
incompatibleKey.cause2: The file was manually modified or partially uploaded
incompatibleKey.cause3: The upload was interrupted during setup
incompatibleKey.cause4: Files were migrated from another provider to the wrong location
incompatibleKey.option1Desc: Delete the OwnJournal folder from {{provider}} and reconnect. This will create a new encryption key.
incompatibleKey.option2Desc: If you want to investigate, download and inspect the encryption key file:
incompatibleKey.option3Desc: If you recently migrated from another provider, the encryption key may be in the wrong location. Check your cloud storage and move the file:
incompatibleKey.requiredFields: The file should contain these fields:
